title: ‘Technical glitch’ delays CUET-UG 2026 exam across several centres
content: Noting that the issue has since been resolved, NTA said that the “exam is being conducted with full compensatory time so that no candidate is disadvantaged”

title: Pattabiram residents demand limited-use subway beneath ROB
content: Though one carriageway of the six-lane Rail Over Bridge (ROB) was opened to traffic in September 2024, no provision has been made for slow-moving traffic that also need to cross Level Crossing No. 2 (Railway Siding) at Pattabiram

title: Portion of platform shed collapses at Lucknow station, three injured
content: Sources said renovation and concourse construction work was underway at the station and portions of the platform number five shed had already been dismantled. The collapsed section was also slated for removal

title: Gen. Chauhan wraps up ‘very satisfying’ tenure as CDS; Lt. Gen. Subramani to take charge on May 31
content: As Chief of Defence Staff, Gen. Chauhan played a key role in planning and implementation of Operation Sindoor along with the three service chiefs.

title: Three women devotees, four children faint in Thirupparankundram temple queue as Vaikasi Visakam draws large crowd
content: With the temperature hovering around 34 degrees Celsius, some women carrying infants expressed uneasiness and discomfort, having arrived at the temple as early as 6 a.m.

title: ED provisionally attaches ₹634.12 crore assets linked to Noida-based UGCC project
content: The ED probe is based on 76 First Information Reports registered by the Delhi Police and CBI against Unitech Limited, its promoters/directors and others, alleging that they fraudulently diverted homebuyers’ funds

title: Kerala HC extends stay on arrest of viral Kumbh Mela woman’s husband Farmaan Khan
content: An FIR was registered against Farmaan Khan, and a probe was launched by the National Commission for Scheduled Tribe after the woman’s father claimed that she was minor

title: Report warns that cybercriminals are exploiting Cockroach Janta Party’s popularity to con GenZ
content: The 33-page report flagged a fake Android app posing as CJP’s official app as a malware threat capable of hacking devices and stealing user data

title: Patient found dead at GGH Guntur; Police launch investigation
content: Hospital sources said the patient had reportedly gone for morning ablutions and was later found lying injured on the ground floor of the building. The reasons behind this extreme step have yet to be ascertained

title: Pune hooch tragedy: 22 police, Excise personnel face suspension
content: Eight persons, including a key bootlegger, have been arrested in connection with the tragedy that unfolded across Pimpri-Chinchwad and Pune city over a span of two days

title: CBI files chargesheet against Shubhkamna Buildtech Private Limited, directors in cheating case
content: The CBI probe revealed that the accused builder company and its directors induced innocent homebuyers and investors through false assurances, misleading representations and deceptive promises, causing wrongful loss to the victims

title: International Peacekeepers Day: India underscores unflinching commitment to UN peacekeeping
content: The International Day of United Nations Peacekeepers is marked annually on May 29, and India is among the largest troop-contributing countries which has deployed nearly three lakh troops in more than 50 UN Peacekeeping Missions since 1948

title: Vizhinjam seaport in Kerala becomes fastest Indian port to handle 2 million TEUs cargo in 18 months
content: Port is expected to commission its first 400-metre berth of total 1.2 km berth expansion conceived as part of second phase expansion in 2027, which will enable it to accommodate more ship calls and handle higher cargo volumes

title: Multilateral joint military exercise in Meghalaya focuses on counter-insurgency, building trust
content: Over 400 soldiers, including around 20 women, from 13 other countries participated in the programme along with the India Army

title: Indian court ruling on Google keyword ads could reshape online advertising
content: The court ordered Google to pay ‌damages of $31,600 in a ruling issued on May 22 by the ‌Delhi High Court

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 | Why football’s most passionate fans may be in India
content: In Kerala, West Bengal and several other places across India, the FIFA World Cup is a homecoming — of fan clubs, midnight screenings, bike rallies and WhatsApp groups that spring back to life every four years

title: Bengaluru rain: Vijayanagar Club compound wall collapses, kills 52-year-old auto driver
content: The victim is an auto driver who had brought his mother and wife to the hospital, dropped them off at the entrance, and driven to the next lane to avoid a traffic pile-up

title: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari launches cervical cancer vaccination drive for over 7.5 lakh girls aged 14 years
content: Under the campaign, girls who have completed 14 years of age but are yet to turn 15 will receive a single dose of the quadrivalent Gardasil-4 vaccine free of cost

title: K.P. Dhanapalan, senior Congress leader and former Kerala MP, passes away
content: A prominent face of the Congress in Ernakulam, Mr. Dhanapalan was elected to the Lok Sabha from Chalakudy constituency in 2009

title: Destruction of rocks at Defence land in Ammuguda decried
content: Ammuguda hillocks are home to ancient rock formations and open scrub lands which are used by the society members and local residents for trekking, and rock climbing activities

title: Operation Sindoor defined India’s response to provocation, says Army chief
content: Calling the occasion ‘a poignant and a particularly personal moment’, General Upendra Dwivedi recalled passing out from the same quarterdeck over 42 years ago

title: T.U. Kuruvilla, former Kerala PWD Minister, dies
content: An active member of the Kerala Congress since its formative years, he was part for the V.S. Achuthanandan Cabinet in 2006

title: Dedicated policy framework proposed for Sivakasi’s ₹6,000-crore fireworks industry
content: Minister for Industries S. Keerthana says the reform aims to further formalise the sector through specific and targeted measures that address multiple needs of all stakeholders

title: Myanmar President Aung Hlaing begins India visit
content: Mr. Aung Hlaing kicked off his trip by landing in Gaya to visit the Mahabodhi temple, ahead of his arrival in Delhi

title: Himachal panchayat elections: Over 19% polling recorded till 9 a.m. in final phase
content: The Sirmaur district had the highest voter turnout at 23.60%, while Lahaul and Spiti registered the lowest at 16.23%

title: Kerala Lok Ayukatha seeks powers to initiate Contempt of Court proceedings, draft rules pending for over one year
content: The draft of the Kerala Lok Ayuktha Contempt of Courts Rules 2025 were submitted to the previous LDF government

title: Kochi Corporation submits nine priority projects to Kerala CM for State support
content: Projects to address traffic congestion, drinking water shortage, and waste management are among those included in the list

title: Elephant calf rescued after falling into water tank near Anthiyur in T.N.
content: Rescue personnel first pumped out the water from the tank using an electric motor. A JCB excavator was then pressed into service to breach a portion of the structure and create a passage for the animal to climb out

title: Nagaland yields a new-to-science cascade frog species
content: Recorded from Kiphire district, the frog has been named Amolops kamal to honour the mentor of one of the six scientists involved in the study

title: Heavy rain and thunderstorms batter Kolkata and surrounding
content: Air, rail, and metro services hit as a squall passes over Kolkata; several cars across Kolkata and Salt Lake were crushed under uprooted trees

title: India’s 2022 forensic law marks shift from colonial-era framework, says National Law University VC
content: The 24th Triennial meeting of the International Association of Forensic Sciences being held from May 25 to 30, brought together forensic experts, legal scholars and investigators from across the world

title: Pahalgam after the terror attack: Why tourists are returning to Kashmir’s most beautiful hill station
content: It is over one year since militants unleashed carnage in Kashmir’s Pahalgam, killing 26 tourists. What followed is a revenge operation dyed in vermillion and an unsettling silence in the ‘Switzerland of India’. Today, the streets of Pahalgam, seem to cajole travellers to come back. Leave fear and prejudice at the border, and sip on some kahwa instead

title: Infosys CEO's compensation rises 2.5% to nearly $8.7 million in fiscal 2026
content: Earlier this month, Indian IT stocks fell to their lowest point in three years after OpenAI announced a new services-led venture

title: Kochi faces waterlogging in many areas in heavy overnight showers
content: Ernakulam South railway station, Kalathiparambil Road, Karikkamuri, KSRTC stand, Ernakulam North, Edakochi, Palluruthy, Panayappilly, and Edappally were among the areas affected

title: Switzerland to collaborate with J&K on sustainable tourism, vocational training
content: Switzerland’s Ambassador to India announced that the country “will continue to work together in the region, from a Himalayan glaciology collaborative program at the University of Kashmir to cloud research with ETH Zurich”

title: 89 additional CAPF units, advanced surveillance, real-time monitoring for safe Amarnath yatra this year
content: Police officials said J&K Director General of Police Nalin Prabhat directed the district heads “to ensure proper arrangements for incoming units and their efficient ground deployment”

title: Man killed in wild elephant attack in Kerala’s Athirappilly; protest hartal called
content: 65-year-old attacked near his home in Vaisyeri; locals demand urgent action as human-wildlife conflict intensifies

title: Fire engulfs Hyderabad-Kakinada private bus on NH-65; 36 passengers escape
content: The bus, operated by Rainbow Travels, had departed from Miyapur in Hyderabad and was en route to Kakinada when a suspected short circuit triggered a fire in the front portion of the vehicle

title: Senior citizen’s caretaker, accomplice arrested on charge of robbing gold and valuables in Belagavi
content: The caretaker allegedly stole the valuables in small portions, and deposited them in a private firm in exchange for gold loans

title: Manoj Jarange Patil firm to launch indefinite hunger strike from May 30
content: Manoj Jarange Patil dismissed the claims of 58 lakhs Kunbi entries being a misunderstanding by BJP Minister Radhakrishna Vikhe Patil, saying that these figures were announced by the government itself

title: Tiger ‘Explorer’ walks back to Papikonda National Park
content: It was inching closer to Yeleru reservoir from the Pampa reservoir through dense forest cover in the Kakinada district by Tuesday night.

title: Maharastra tiger Explorer returns to East Godavari again
content: The tiger has crossed the Godavari River twice after it was released into Papikonda National Park on February 16, say officials

title: Kolhapur forms team of 10 transwomen for disaster management and rescue work
content: The disaster management cell of the State government has claimed that it is the first such initiative in Maharashtra

title: Spurious liquor kills 15 in Pune, Pimpri-Chinchwad; 8 held, probe ordered
content: Maharashtra CM orders police commissioners to conduct a joint probe; raids conducted at multiple liquor shops overnight; Opposition leaders allege failure in law enforcement, accuse police of a cover-up

title: Worth examining Vijay’s appeal to children to convince adults to vote for him, says judge citing personal anecdote
content: He opines that a case filed against TVK for making an appeal to children during elections is worth being looked into

title: AAP wins most wards in Punjab local polls, Opposition alleges misuse of govt. machinery
content: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann says AAP’s victory in the municipal elections is a decisive endorsement of his government’s development-driven governance model

title: Indian Staff At US Hotel Had Sex With Trafficked Teen For Cheaper Room Rates, Jailed
content: Kavankumar Patel, a resident of Gujarat, was an employee at a hotel in Omaha and assaulted the girl in January last year.

title: Blue Origin Rocket Explosion Is Bad News For Both Jeff Bezos And NASA
content: Space exploration is filled with setbacks, but the spectacular explosion of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket on Thursday night marked a significant blow to not only the company, which was founded by billionaire Jeff Bezos, but also NASA, with the...

title: What To Know About Manhattanhenge, New York's Sunset Spectacle
content: The traditional viewing spots are along the city's broad east-west thoroughfares: 14th Street, 23rd Street, 34th Street, 42nd Street and 57th Street.

title: No Visibility, Narrow Tunnels: 3-Nation Laos Cave Rescue Team Faces Hurdles
content: Rescue teams had pumped water out of the flooded cave's passages, but a morning rainstorm complicated their work.

title: Tomatoes Become Latest Symbol Of America's Affordability Squeeze
content: Prices for those red orbs have soared more than any other food product over the past year to cement a spot as one of the consumer headaches du jour.

title: Russian Spies Aggressively Seek Western Technology As Sanctions Bite
content: Four years of international sanctions have hampered Moscow's ability to procure machinery, technology and research from Europe.

title: Canadian Poison Seller Pleads Guilty To Aiding Suicides, Avoids Murder Trial
content: The Canadian man who sold poison to distressed people worldwide pleaded guilty on Friday to 14 counts of aiding suicide but avoided a murder trial, an outcome one...

title: Trump's In 'Excellent Health', But Body Mass Index Nears Obese Level
content: Trump had recently talked about how good he feels, even as he joked about his fondness for fast food and his minimal exercise beyond frequent golf outings.

title: "Modernising Military With High-End Ops": Top Trump Aide's Praise For India
content: "India is powerful and modernising its military," Pete Hegseth said. He also highlighted that it is maintaining a balance of power, particularly in the Indian Ocean.

title: No Red Lines, No Deal: Trump's Iran Meet Ends Without "Final Determination"
content: Trump had earlier said in a post on Truth Social that Iran will clear mines it laid in the Strait of Hormuz, US will lift its naval blockade and ships will start moving.

title: 64% Indian Women Now Use Internet, Up From 33% In 2019-21: National Survey
content: The NFHS-5 for the 2019-2021 period had recorded that 33.3% Indian women had used the internet at least once. This figure has doubled over the interim two-year period

title: Iran War LIVE Updates: Trump's Meet With Aides Ends Without "Final Determination"
content: US Iran War LIVE Updates: US President Donald Trump had on Wednesday dismissed an Iranian state media report that Iran and Oman would jointly manage shipping through the Strait of Hormuz

title: "Never Seen Anything Like It": Satellite Pics Show China Is Building Launch Pads Near Nuclear Missile Silos
content: Satellite images show Beijing is building launch pads, bunkers and communications nodes near the isolated nuclear silos that hold the Chinese military's longest-range missiles.

title: Vice Admiral Ajay Kochhar Takes Charge As 48th Vice Chief Of Naval Staff
content: An alumnus of the prestigious National Defence Academy, Pune, Vice Admiral Ajay Kochhar was commissioned into the Indian Navy on July 1, 1988.

title: RBI's Currency Makeover: Why Polymer Notes Are Important
content: Unlike paper currency, polymer notes are more durable and resistant to dirt and moisture, factors that are especially relevant in India's climate and usage conditions.

title: Weather Today LIVE: Temperatures Dip In Many States, Weather Body Flags Below-Normal Monsoon
content: Weather Updates Today: Delhi remained under cloudy skies, logging its lowest maximum temperature in past 21 days. The IMD has forecast rain and thunderstorms across parts of north India, including Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, UP, Uttarakhand.

title: Karnataka Politics Live Updates: Key Congress Meet Today On Cabinet Reshuffle
content: Karnataka Politics Live Updates: A change in leadership in the three-year-old Karnataka government appears imminent, with Siddaramaiah having sought an appointment with Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot, reportedly to tender his resignation.

title: 'India preparing for Operation Sindoor 2.0': Army chief Upendra Dwivedi
content: India is preparing for 'Operation Sindoor 2.0' as Army chief Upendra Dwivedi stated the military posture in response to Pakistan's actions remains in place. The operation, a continuation of last year's retaliatory response to terrorism, involves enhancing synergy among all three services for future warfare. The focus is on cautious deployment and troop protection in border areas.

title: 'PM Modi supervised NEET paper leak': Rahul's big charge as Centre gears up for retest
content: Rahul Gandhi sharply criticized Prime Minister Modi over the NEET UG paper leak, accusing him of personally supervising the exam's integrity. This follows the Centre's assurance to the Supreme Court about the upcoming re-examination. The apex court expressed concern for students, while the NTA detailed extensive security reforms and new safeguards implemented to prevent future irregularities.

title: 'It hurts deeply': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi shares RR's post after IPL 2026 exit
content: Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 campaign ended in heartbreak after a Qualifier 2 loss to Gujarat Titans. Despite a brilliant 96 from 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, the team fell short. Sooryavanshi's stellar season, marked by maturity and exceptional batting against top bowlers, has earned him high praise and suggestions of an imminent India call-up.

title: 'India is powerful and modernising its military': US defence secretary Pete Hegseth
content: US defence secretary Pete Hegseth lauded India's military modernization and strategic importance in the Indo-Pacific. He highlighted New Delhi as a critical anchor for regional balance and praised its growing industrial capacity for high-end military operations.  The US is pursuing co-production initiatives with India, including for Javelin munitions, to enhance collective readiness.

title: As Wix lays off 1,000 employees, CEO says cuts were the 'right decision'
content: Wix is laying off approximately 20% of its workforce, impacting around 1,000 employees. This significant restructuring is driven by the strengthening Israeli shekel and the rapid evolution of AI capabilities. CEO Avishai Abrahami stated the move is necessary to create a faster, leaner, and flatter organization.

title: Need low-cost housing? Here's how the SPARSH 2.0 scheme works
content: The Union Territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu has launched SPARSH 2.0, a new housing initiative to boost affordable rental homes for workers and the economically weaker sections. Government agencies and PSUs are encouraged to build or convert complexes into low-cost rental units, aiming to enhance living conditions and support the 'Housing for All' mission.

title: No one-man show: How RCB became a complete unit
content: RCB's Director of Cricket Mo Bobat believes the franchise's new identity matches with Rajat Patidar's. Built on fearless intent and collective contributions, RCB's second consecutive title push is strengthened by the experience of Virat Kohli, Krunal Pandya, Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood. They will face Gujarat Titans in the final on Sunday.

title: Trump's medical report: US president 'fully fit' to work but asked to shed weight
content: President Trump's latest medical report declared him in 'excellent health' and fit to serve, despite advising weight loss and increased physical activity. The report noted his weight has increased, and he takes medication for cholesterol and cardiac prevention. Bruising on his hands was attributed to frequent handshaking while on aspirin.

title: Microsoft CEO's Copilot ‘confusion fix’ that left employees laughing in townhall
content: Microsoft is reportedly developing a 'super app' to consolidate its confusing array of Copilot AI tools. This unified platform aims to end customer frustration by offering a single destination for various AI assistants, including coding, chat, and collaboration features. The app, expected by summer's end, will allow seamless switching between personal and work accounts, simplifying Microsoft's AI ecosystem.

title: Ajith Kumar's mother Mohini Mani passes away
content: Tamil cinema's beloved star Ajith Kumar is grappling with profound personal sorrow following the demise of his mother, Mohini Mani. She passed away in Chennai due to age-related health complications. The actor, reportedly traveling abroad at the time, is now making arrangements to be with his grieving family.

title: 'Amazon would collapse if run like New York City': Jeff Bezos takes swipe Mamdani
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has endorsed New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's new government efficiency initiative, COGE. This comes after their public disagreement on taxing the wealthy. Bezos, who previously criticized city spending, now supports Mamdani's plan to make government work smarter, suggesting savings could benefit lower earners.

title: IPL captaincy churn: Pant steps down, Pandya may be on way out
content: LSG had made Rishabh Pant captain after bidding Rs 27 crore for him, making him the most expensive player in IPL history. On the other hand, MI stirred a controversy when they brought Hardik Pandya back to their fold from Gujarat Titans and named him captain, replacing Rohit Sharma, then India’s captain across all three formats.

title: IP University admission 2026: GGSIPU application window closing, direct link to apply here
content: Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University's extended online application window for admissions closes today, May 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM. Prospective students must apply online, uploading necessary documents and test scores. Admissions are based on national tests and qualifying merit, with registrations needing completion to avoid last-minute issues.

title: 'Bakra halal hote dekha hai ... ': Ghaziabad teen stabbed to death
content: A 17-year-old boy, Surya Pratap, died after being stabbed multiple times by his friend Asad in Ghaziabad's Khoda Colony. The incident occurred following an argument, with the accused fleeing the scene. Police are investigating the motive and have formed teams to apprehend Asad.

title: RCB: Table-toppers, most consistent side and favourites to defend title
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru are on the cusp of defending their IPL title after a dominant IPL 2026 campaign. Under captain Rajat Patidar, RCB finished top of the league and stormed into the final with a crushing win in Qualifier 1. They now face Gujarat Titans, looking like strong favourites to secure back-to-back championships.

title: 'Differing opinions shouldn't shock': RSS leader on Cockroach Janata Party buzz
content: RSS Prachar Pramukh Sunil Ambekar downplayed concerns about the satirical "Cockroach Janata Party" (CJP), stating that open debate and differing views are normal in a democratic society. He emphasized India's aware populace, transparent elections, free media, and active public discourse, asserting that institutions and political parties are capable of handling such discussions independently.

title: 'ICE arrest map': White House launches bizarre new 'alien-themed' immigration website
content: The White House launched a new space-themed website, Aliens.gov, to highlight immigration enforcement actions. Using extraterrestrial imagery, the site aims to showcase arrests of undocumented immigrants and promote President Trump's agenda. It features a live dashboard and interactive map of enforcement activities, drawing criticism for its dehumanizing language.

title: From logistics parks to offices: How is Middle East conflict hitting Asia's property market
content: The Middle East conflict is impacting Asia Pacific's commercial real estate through rising energy costs and inflation. This is affecting logistics, retail, and office sectors, while investment markets face growing uncertainty due to potential interest rate shifts. Broader macroeconomic channels are expected to shape the region's property outlook.

title: 'If namaz on road wrong, ban all festivals': Owaisi points at 'double standard'
content: AIMIM chief Asaduddin Owaisi criticized what he termed a double standard regarding religious practices, stating that if offering namaz on roads is deemed wrong, then restrictions should apply equally to all faiths, citing Article 25 of the Constitution. He questioned why objections are raised against Muslim prayers but not religious processions of other communities.

title: The Rs 7 cr bargain vs the Rs 25 cr gamble: How Holder outshone IPL 2026's biggest buys
content: IPL auctions are often won on perception in December, but the real story usually emerges months later. While franchises splashed massive sums on Cameron Green, Matheesha Pathirana and Liam Livingstone, the smartest buys of IPL 2026 quietly came from the Rs 7-9 crore bracket. Jason Holder topped the charts by a distance, Josh Inglis maximised limited opportunities, and Ravi Bishnoi delivered steady returns. Meanwhile, several marquee signings struggled with injuries, poor form and role mismatches, turning auction-night hype into underwhelming seasons.

title: AP SBTET diploma results 2026 released for C23, C20, C16 schemes ; direct download link here
content: AP SBTET Diploma Results 2026: The AP SBTET has released the 2026 diploma examination results for C23, C20, C16, C14 engineering schemes and ER-91, ER-2020 pharmacy regulations on May 29. Students can access provisional marksheets online through the official AP SBTET portal and other education websites using their PIN or Hall Ticket number. The results cover multiple semesters, including supplementary exams. The board will soon issue original marks memos and announce details regarding recounting and re-verification procedures.

title: Is Indus Treaty deadlock hurting Pak? Karachi reels under chronic water shortage
content: Karachi faces a severe water crisis during peak summer, coinciding with Eid-ul-Adha. Jamaat-e-Islami Pakistan chief Hafiz Naeem ur Rehman has accused the ruling PPP of gross mismanagement and failure to address the city's chronic water shortages despite 18 years in power.  Nearly 70% of the city is reportedly experiencing extended supply disruptions, forcing residents to buy expensive private tankers.

title: Deepak Tijori breaks silence on Rahul Roy's health
content: Deepak Tijori has refuted recent rumors concerning his 'Aashiqui' co-star Rahul Roy's health, stating they are in regular contact and Rahul is "absolutely safe, absolutely fine." Tijori expressed disappointment over the spread of inaccurate celebrity news, a situation he has personally experienced. He emphasized that such reports often lack factual basis and are an unavoidable aspect of their profession.

title: Rain havoc in Bengaluru: Man killed after wall crashes on auto
content: A Bengaluru autorickshaw driver died tragically when a private club's compound wall collapsed on his parked vehicle during heavy rain and strong winds. Shivaboraiah was trapped and crushed by the debris. Police are investigating the cause of the wall's collapse.

title: CNG price hiked to Rs 86 per kg in Mumbai, piped gas to Rs 52 per unit
content: Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) prices have increased by Rs 2 per kg in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, taking the new rate to Rs 86 per kg. Piped cooking gas will also see a 50 paise per unit hike. This rise is attributed to global energy supply chain disruptions and increased procurement costs.

title: Nagpur man slips, dies in house; bedridden wife starves to death amid extreme heat
content: A 77-year-old retired defence employee and his bedridden wife were found dead in their locked Nagpur home. The husband likely died first after a fall, leaving his wife to succumb days later to dehydration and starvation in the extreme summer heat. Their tragic end highlights a painful and lonely death due to lack of immediate support.

title: From Ahinda to Mekedatu: 5 challenges before Karnataka's new CM
content: Karnataka's new Congress government, led by DK Shivakumar, faces a critical transition. Key challenges include cabinet formation balancing caste and regional demands, sustaining welfare guarantees amid fiscal strain, and navigating the sensitive Mekedatu reservoir dispute with Tamil Nadu. Shivakumar must also retain the crucial Ahinda support base and ensure development extends beyond Bengaluru to rural areas.

title: Mumbai Indians concerned about numerous dressing room 'leaks'
content: Mumbai Indians faced a dismal IPL 2026, plagued by persistent dressing room unrest and damaging leaks of sensitive information. This internal turmoil, escalating since Hardik Pandya's captaincy, has frustrated franchise figures and insiders alike. Management is now prioritising an immediate crackdown on these breaches to restore the team's former glory.

title: Gujarat-origin man pleads guilty in $9.5m gambling racket in US
content: A Gujarat-origin man residing in the US has pleaded guilty to a multimillion-dollar illegal gambling conspiracy in southwest Missouri. Tushar Patel admitted his role in a racket that generated nearly $9.5 million in illegal proceeds. He faces charges including conspiracy to commit wire fraud, operate an illegal gambling business, and money laundering.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks down in dugout after Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 exit
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's IPL 2026 dream ended in tears despite his stellar 96, as Gujarat Titans advanced to the final.  The young Rajasthan Royals star, who amassed 776 runs this season, was visibly heartbroken after his team's elimination.  Captain Riyan Parag lauded his exceptional talent and maturity, predicting a bright future for the prodigious cricketer.

title: Gulf's other war: UAE carried out strikes on Iran despite ceasefire — Report
content: The UAE's involvement in the Middle East conflict was deeper than previously known, with coordinated strikes on Iran, including energy facilities, even after an April ceasefire. These actions exposed divisions within the Gulf, as Saudi Arabia expressed concerns about escalating risks to regional energy supplies and global markets.

title: Ranveer Singh 'Don 3' row: Ram Gopal Varma questions FWICE action, says ‘it’s a kangaroo court’
content: Filmmaker Ram Gopal Varma has publicly supported Ranveer Singh amidst the 'Don 3' controversy, criticizing the FWICE's non-cooperation directive. Varma argued that the FWICE lacks authority and that stars like Singh are crucial to the industry's existence and employment, not the federation.

title: Sikh man’s murder conviction in UK triggers storm over carrying kirpan in public
content: A British Sikh man was convicted of murder using a kirpan, sparking calls to ban the ceremonial dagger in public. Accusations of two-tier policing arose after officers handcuffed the dying victim, believing a false claim of racial abuse. The victim's mother was convicted of aiding the killer. Investigations into police conduct are underway.

title: Tamannaah Bhatia's old temple video sparks attention
content: Actress Tamannaah Bhatia's recent temple visit in Mumbai has gone viral, showcasing her spiritual side. Previously, she shared how Isha Yoga Centre practices, particularly meditation, have profoundly impacted her life, leading to increased joy and purpose. Tamannaah emphasizes spirituality's role in achieving happiness independent of external circumstances, encouraging others to explore its benefits.

title: 'Poison gas, wear mask, no water': Man dies by suicide, leaves poignant note behind
content: A 42-year-old man, struggling with unemployment and financial distress, allegedly died by suicide in Gandhinagar. He left a note warning his family about poisonous substances in the house, showing concern for his daughter's safety. Police suspect the death occurred around May 22, with toxic pesticide packets recovered from the scene.

title: Four lines that 'layoff email' from Meta Leadership has for those on H-1B visa
content: Meta has initiated another round of significant layoffs, impacting nearly 8,000 employees globally, with notifications starting in Singapore. While many are let go, over 7,000 will transition to AI initiatives. The company is providing immigration guidance and severance packages, including 16 weeks' pay plus two weeks per service year, and 18 months of COBRA coverage for affected staff.

title: Trump will accept only Iran deal that meets his red lines: White House
content: President Trump has yet to approve a proposed deal with Iran, emphasizing that any agreement must meet his conditions, including preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons. While discussions continue, Iran denies negotiating under US dictates. The uncertainty impacts energy markets, with regional tensions also escalating between Iran, the US, and Israel-Hezbollah.

title: Karnataka: CLP to elect DK Shivakumar leader today, paving way for CM post
content: DK Shivakumar is set to be formally elected as the new leader of the Congress Legislature Party in Bengaluru, succeeding Chief Minister Siddaramaiah. AICC leaders will attend the meeting where Shivakumar will be elected, followed by his submission of the CLP resolution to the governor to stake his claim in forming the new cabinet.

title: Operation Numkhor: Key aide of Biswadip Das arrested
content: your active subscription(s) Account subscription benefits alongside Premium Stories, Editorials, Opinions and more . Unlock these with Subscription Products you've access to Additional Subscription Benefits Account Settings Need help with your subscription? May 30, 2026 e-Paper The View From India Looking at World Affairs from the Indian perspective .

title: 'Maligning isn't constructive criticism': HC on Madhu Kishwar's bail plea
content: a u.s. prosecutor submitted the anticipatory bail plea . a video allegedly resembling the holder of a constitutional post was uploaded and circulated on social media . there is a difference between constructive criticism and tweeting to malign, justice says .

title: Indian man Kavankumar Patel jailed after paying human traffickers to abuse minors
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. title: 60% chance of deficient monsoon as IMD downgrades rain forecast
